samples.xml_description:
<module id="pulse_detect">
    <description>Pulse detection module. Detects rising edges of data_in and outputs a pulse.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="clk">Clock signal.</input>
        <input id="rst_n">Reset signal (active low).</input>
        <input id="data_in">One-bit input signal.</input>
        <output id="data_out">Output signal indicating the presence of pulses.</output>
    </ports>
    <parameter_description>
        <state id="INITIAL">Initial state.</state>
        <state id="WAIT_FOR_ONE">Waiting for data_in to go high.</state>
        <state id="WAIT_FOR_ZERO">Waiting for data_in to go low after going high.</state>
        <state id="PULSE_END">End of pulse detected.</state>
    </parameter_description>
    <logic_description type="sequential_logic">
        <reg id="state" type="enum" enum_id="state" default_value="INITIAL"/>
        <reg id="next_state" type="enum" enum_id="state"/>
        <always_comb>
            case(state)
                INITIAL: begin
                    if(data_in == 1'b1)
                        next_state = WAIT_FOR_ZERO;
                    else
                        next_state = WAIT_FOR_ONE;
                end
                WAIT_FOR_ONE: begin
                    if(data_in == 1'b1)
                        next_state = WAIT_FOR_ZERO;
                    else
                        next_state = WAIT_FOR_ONE;
                end
                WAIT_FOR_ZERO: begin
                    if(data_in == 1'b0)
                        next_state = PULSE_END;
                    else
                        next_state = WAIT_FOR_ONE;
                end
                PULSE_END: begin
                    if(data_in == 1'b0)
                        next_state = WAIT_FOR_ONE;
                    else
                        next_state = WAIT_FOR_ZERO;
                end
                default: next_state = INITIAL;
            endcase
        </always_comb>
        
        always @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n) begin
            if(~rst_n) begin
                state <= INITIAL;
            end
            else begin
                state <= next_state;
            end
        end
    </logic_description>
    <implementation>
        // Implement pulse detection logic here
        reg [1:0] state_reg;
        reg data_out_reg;
        always @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n) begin
            if(~rst_n) begin
                state_reg <= 2'b00;
                data_out_reg <= 1'b0;
            end
            else begin
                case(state_reg)
                    2'b00: begin
                        if(data_in == 1'b1)
                            state_reg <= 2'b01;
                        else
                            state_reg <= 2'b00;
                    end
                    2'b01: begin
                        if(data_in == 1'b1)
                            state_reg <= 2'b10;
                        else
                            state_reg <= 2'b00;
                    end
                    2'b10: begin
                        if(data_in == 1'b0)
                            state_reg <= 2'b11;
                        else
                            state_reg <= 2'b01;
                    end
                    2'b11: begin
                        if(data_in == 1'b0)
                            state_reg <= 2'b00;
                        else
                            state_reg <= 2'b10;
                    end
                endcase
            end
        end
        
        always @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n) begin
            if(~rst_n) begin
                data_out_reg <= 1'b0;
            end
            else begin
                if(state_reg == 2'b11)
                    data_out_reg <= 1'b1;
                else
                    data_out_reg <= 1'b0;
            end
        end
    </implementation>
    
endmodule
